introMessage: ## Thank you for adding me to your server! Here's a quick rundown to get started:
- Use the `/alerts` commands to manage weather alert reporting
- Use the `/forecasts` command to designate a channel for reporting hourly forecasts to
- Use the `/auto-radar` command to designate a channel to keep an auto-updating doppler radar animation in
- Use the `/radar` command to get the current doppler radar animation for a location
Admins in this server can use the `/announcement` command to set a channel to receive announcements related to my operation. Announcements are opt-in as to reduce noise.
Lastly, if you encounter any bugs, oddities, or have any suggestions then use the `/feedback` command to submit feedback!
-# I apologize if this is not an appropriate channel for this introduction.

errors.commandFailed: I've encountered a problem executing that command. Please try again later.

softwareBody: I'm built on the following software:
... and I'm hosted on my creator's [Raspberry Pi 5](https://raspberrypi.com/products/raspberry-pi-5) running [Ubuntu Server 24.04](https://ubuntu.com/server).
bot.apiTitle: Where do you get your weather data?
bot.apiBody: I get all of my info from the [National Weather Service API](https://weather.gov/documentation/services-web-api).
bot.aiUseBody: **Nope!** I neither use AI to parse or transform data nor is any of my code written in AI. All of my code is written by a human, mistakes and all!

changelog.responseTooLong: A preview of my commits is currently too large to send. You can view my commits [here](https://github.com/{repo:string}/commits).

created: {channel:string} should now have a placeholder message sent to it that will be periodically updated with the desired radar image loop.
To remove this designation, simply delete the message at any time.
